name: IGF-1 LR3
subtitle: Modified Growth Factor Analog | Muscle Growth
overview: IGF-1 LR3 is a synthetic 83-amino acid analog of insulin-like growth factor-1 that has never been approved for human use. The N-terminal extension and R3 substitution reduce binding protein interaction, maintaining elevated free circulating levels with ~3x greater potency than native IGF-1.
mechanism: Functions as a full IGF-1 receptor agonist activating PI3K/Akt/mTOR and MAPK/ERK pathways. The modifications prevent protein sequestration, maintaining elevated free circulating levels for extended anabolic effects.
keyBenefits: Approximately 3x more potent than native IGF-1
Promotes muscle hypertrophy and hyperplasia in animal models
15-20% lean mass gains observed in 4-week rat studies
Anti-catabolic effects preserve muscle during cachexia

items.name: Hypertrophy
items.description: 15-20% lean mass gains in 4 weeks through satellite cell activation (rat studies).

items.name: Anti-Catabolic
items.description: Cancer cachexia rats maintained 30% more muscle versus placebo.

items.name: Hyperplasia
items.description: Creates new muscle fibers via satellite cell differentiation.

items.name: Wound Healing
items.description: Accelerated wound healing in animal models.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous or intramuscular injection. CRITICAL: Consume 30-60g fast carbohydrates immediately after injection to prevent hypoglycemia. Never inject before sleep.

steps: Inject diluent slowly along vial wall
Store immediately at 2-8°C protected from light
BAC water reconstitution: use within 7 days

interactions.notes: Creates excessive IGF-1 levels; avoid combination.
Both stimulate GH/IGF-1 axis; use caution with combination.
May compound metabolic effects through GH stimulation.
Synergistic hypoglycemic effects can be life-threatening.

timeline.effects: Increased muscle pump and fullness; possible hypoglycemia if carbs inadequate
Enhanced recovery, strength gains, visible fullness, potential water retention
Maximum effects; receptor desensitization approaching; joint stiffness common
Pump loss over 1-2 weeks; strength/size gains may persist with training

stopSigns: Severe or recurring hypoglycemia despite carbohydrate intake
Unusual growths, lumps, or rapid mole changes
Severe joint pain or carpal tunnel symptoms
Persistent nausea, headaches, or vision changes
contraindications: NEVER approved for human use - research chemical only
WADA prohibited - causes failed drug tests
monitoring: CRITICAL: Consume 30-60g fast carbohydrates immediately after injection
Never inject before sleep (overnight hypoglycemia risk)
Monitor blood glucose regularly, especially week one
4-6 week maximum cycle; equal off-period required

good: Certificate of analysis from reputable source
warning: Research chemical status - never approved for human use
bad: Severe hypoglycemia risk - life-threatening low blood sugar lasting 20-30 hours
Cancer proliferation concern - UK Biobank links elevated IGF-1 to multiple cancers
Black market quality varies - oxidized and degraded forms common

references.title: Insulin-like growth factor-I (IGF-I) and especially IGF-I variants are anabolic in dexamethasone-treated rats
references.authors: Tomas FM, Knowles SE, Owens PC, Chandler CS, Francis GL, Read LC, Ballard FJ
references.keyFindings: LR3IGF-I was approximately 2.5-fold more potent than native IGF-1 in reversing dexamethasone-induced catabolism. 7-day treatment showed significant body weight recovery vs 19g loss in controls.

references.title: Attenuated glucose-stimulated insulin secretion during an acute IGF-1 LR3 infusion into fetal sheep does not persist in isolated islets
references.authors: Stremming J, White A, Galan HL, Brown LD
references.journal: American Journal of Physiology - Regulatory, Integrative and Comparative Physiology
references.keyFindings: Fetal plasma insulin concentrations decreased 66% with IGF-1 LR3 infusion during hyperglycemic clamp. Insulin secretion suppression was acute and did not persist in isolated islets.

references.title: Insulin-like growth factor I preserves host lean tissue mass in cancer cachexia
references.authors: Ng EH, Rock CS, Lazarus DD, Stiaino-Coico L, Moldawer LL, Bhatt GR
references.keyFindings: IGF-1 treatment effectively attenuated host muscle protein and lean tissue depletion in a sarcoma model without stimulating tumor growth. Dose-dependent increases in carcass weight and gastrocnemius muscle protein.

references.title: Circulating Insulin-like Growth Factor-I Concentrations and Risk of 30 Cancers: Prospective Analyses in UK Biobank
references.authors: Murphy N, Knuppel A, Papadimitriou N, Martin RM, Tsilidis KK, Brennan P et al.
references.keyFindings: Higher circulating IGF-1 associated with increased risks of colorectal, breast, prostate, and thyroid cancers in the UK Biobank prospective cohort of >395,000 participants.

latestResearch.title: Intranasal long R3 insulin-like growth factor-1 treatment promotes amyloid plaque remodeling in cerebral cortex but fails to preserve cognitive function in male 5XFAD mice
latestResearch.summary: 7-month intranasal LR3-IGF-1 treatment improved body composition and reduced filamentous plaques in cortex of Alzheimer's model mice, but failed to preserve cognitive function. Suggests potential for combinatorial approaches rather than monotherapy.

latestResearch.title: IGF-1 LR3 does not promote growth in late-gestation growth-restricted fetal sheep
latestResearch.summary: 1-week IGF-1 LR3 treatment did not improve growth in growth-restricted fetal sheep. Amino acid concentrations decreased in LR3 group, highlighting species- and context-specific responses.

quickStats.frequency: Once daily, or split AM/PM for higher doses
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: -20°C to -80°C. Reconstituted in acetic acid: 2-8°C for 1 year. Reconstituted in BAC water: use within 7 days
